description:
## Goal

Replace per-callsite spec minting with one canonical spec per fn,
keyed on the LUB (element-wise Descr::union) of every observed
input. Track per-slot inflow histograms alongside the LUB so the
statistic that proves correctness today can drive refinement-
emission and inlining later — one mechanism, three downstream
consumers.

The canonical IS the type-theoretic soundness floor. Where
callers are monomorphic, the LUB stays narrow and ArgRepr
projects to RawInt/RawF64 naturally. Where callers mix, the LUB
widens to Tagged. No separate refinement machinery, no router,
no per-target emission policy in v1.

## Background

Today's typer mints a fresh spec per distinct value-narrowed call
site. For interpreter-style programs (ast_eval_env: eval, lookup
walking nested AST tuples) this produces hundreds of specs from a
33-line input. The matrix wall is dominated by Cranelift
debug-codegen-per-spec on one fixture.

fz-aiz.7 already trimmed dead any-key bodies by annotation-driven
reachable_specs seeding (~5244 golden lines removed). But the
underlying spec proliferation persists: the typer's per-callsite
discipline is the source, not the emission policy.

Set-theoretic types literature (CDuce, Elixir 2024) treats
specialization as a downstream optimization, not part of typing —
one canonical body per fn, typed under the LUB. fz should match.

## What lands

- src/ir_typer.rs:
  - New data model:
    ```
    Canonical {
        fid: FnId,
        sid: SpecId,
        slots: Vec<CanonicalSlot>,    // one per fn-entry param
    }
    CanonicalSlot {
        descr: Descr,                  // the LUB
        inflows: HashMap<ArgRepr, usize>,  // RawInt: 99, Tagged: 1, ...
    }
    ```
    The Canonical's `key` (Vec<Descr>) for SpecRegistry indexing
    is just `slots.iter().map(|s| s.descr.clone()).collect()`.

  - `walk_spec_for_discovery` becomes a LUB-accumulator. At each
    push_key for callee F, compute K from args:
    - Merge K[i] into F.slots[i].descr via Descr::union.
    - Increment F.slots[i].inflows[ArgRepr::from_descr(K[i])].
    - If F.slots[i].descr changed, re-walk F's body.
  - Per-fn registry: one entry per fn, replacing today's many.
  - SCC + widen() at WIDEN_AT=3 still drives fixpoint termination
    — same machinery, same threshold.

- src/spec_registry.rs:
  - SpecIds remain flat for annotation indexing; each fn maps to
    exactly one SpecId.
  - resolve() collapses to single-tier FnId lookup. Existing
    subsumption-minimal logic retires — only one spec to match.

- src/ir_codegen.rs:
  - Codegen reads canonical-only. ABI per slot derives from
    ArgRepr::from_descr(canonical.slots[i].descr). No more
    parallel ABI bookkeeping.
  - Annotation arc unchanged — resolved_sid points at the
    canonical's SpecId.
  - reachable_specs simplifies: canonical of every transitively-
    reachable FnId is reachable. No special seeds.

- Diagnostic surface: --Wspec-quality (fz-aiz.6) gains access to
  the per-slot inflow histogram. A slot with `{Tagged: 1, RawInt: 99}`
  is reportable as 'consider splitting this fn: 99% RawInt
  callers paying Tagged tax for 1 polymorphic caller.'

## What the histogram gives us downstream

Three uses, only the first active in v1:

1. **Correctness invariant (v1)**: every contributing ArgRepr in
   a slot's inflows must project from a Descr that's ⊆ the slot's
   LUB. Stats prove the LUB construction is consistent. Debug-
   assert at typer completion.

2. **Refinement-emission trigger (deferred)**: a slot with a
   dominant narrower-repr inflow → mint a refinement keyed on
   that repr. Threshold tunable. Trigger is a count, not a
   heuristic predicate.

3. **Inlining candidate (further deferred)**: a slot's
   singleton-count caller is a textbook inline candidate.
   Inlining may remove the contribution from the LUB, allowing
   canonical itself to project to a narrower repr.

Each downstream consumer reads the same statistic. No re-typing
needed when we flip on (2) or (3); just add the consumer.

## What gets removed

- `fn ensure_any_keys` — canonical IS the floor by construction.
- `naturally_opaque: HashSet<FnId>` — every reachable FnId has a
  canonical that handles opaque dispatch.
- `tagged_return_specs`, `tagged_slot0_cont_specs`,
  `tagged_return_fns` HashMaps — per-spec ABI derives from the
  canonical's slots via ArgRepr::from_descr.
- MakeClosure-target blanket reachable seed in reachable_specs.
- The two-tier SpecRegistry::resolve (exact + subsumption-minimal)
  — collapses to single-tier FnId → SpecId.
- The parallel any-key body story — canonical is the any-key.
- Per-callsite key reconstruction at codegen emit time.
- Approximately 64 references across ir_typer.rs + ir_codegen.rs
  disappear with the bookkeeping maps.

## Expected effect

CLIF function count drops to ~1 per fn + helper conts:
- fib_tailrec: 14 → ~5
- ast_eval_env: 59 → ~10
- list_primitives: similar collapse

fixture_matrix wall time (debug): 60s → ~10-15s.

CLIF goldens shrink by majority. Same goldens for JIT and AOT
(no divergence in v1).

Performance: narrow ABI preserved naturally where callers are
monomorphic (fib's canonical = [int, int, int] → RawInt). Mixed-
caller fns project to Tagged — acceptable v1 baseline, BEAM-tier
for the affected paths.

## fz-t45 dissolves

Closure values' body_fn_id wires to canonical's FuncId. Indirect
dispatch sig = all-Tagged i64. Canonical ABI = Tagged by
construction (LUB includes opaque callers). Wire matches
semantics. TailCallClosure ABI mismatch becomes structurally
impossible. Close fz-t45 when this lands.

## Honest tradeoffs

- `fib(0, _, 1)` loses its constant-fold spec. Goes through
  canonical's body, hits clause 0's pattern match, returns 1 in a
  few instructions instead of zero. Acceptable; if a hot path
  shows this matters, file a targeted refinement follow-up using
  the histogram as the trigger.
- Multi-clause fns with one narrow caller per clause may pay one
  extra branch vs today's narrow-spec model. Same answer:
  measurable regression → targeted follow-up.

## Deferred (file only if measurable need arises)

- Refinement emission driven by inflow histogram (consumer #2
  above).
- Inlining driven by singleton inflow counts (consumer #3).
- Dispatch-prelude router on canonical for opaque entry into
  narrow refinements.
- Per-target (JIT vs AOT) emission policy and golden split.

These all read the histogram. No re-architecture needed when
they ship.

## Acceptance

- All 618 unit + 166 fixture-matrix tests pass with regenerated
  goldens.
- Spec count in expected.specs collapses to ~= fn count.
- CLIF count in expected.clif similarly collapses.
- fixture_matrix debug wall ≤ 20s.
- Per-slot inflow histograms populate during typing; debug-assert
  invariant holds (every contributing ArgRepr projects from a
  Descr ⊆ the slot LUB).
- --Wspec-quality reports inflow histograms cleanly.
- fz-t45 repro program (each + closure with non-empty captures)
  produces correct output `11 / 12 / 13`. Close fz-t45.
- No clippy warnings; fmt clean.

## See also

- fz-aiz.7 — annotation-driven reachable_specs (landed).
- fz-aiz.3a-c — annotation arc (stays intact).
- fz-aiz.6 — spec-quality diagnostic (consumes histogram).
- src/ir_codegen.rs:782 — ArgRepr::from_descr.
title: fz-rfn: canonical-first — one spec per fn, LUB-keyed
